evidence_text: MdNAC18.1 is treated as a core ripening and harvest-date regulator in this curated apple breeding RAG layer. It links the major Chr03 harvest-date GWAS/QTL region with fruit ripening, ethylene-related maturation, and downstream cell-wall softening programs. Evidence type: GWAS/QTL-supported candidate gene and transcription-factor mechanism. Trait: harvest date, ripening time, fruit maturity.

MdACS1 encodes 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylate synthase, a key rate-limiting enzyme for ethylene biosynthesis in apple fruit. ACS1 controls ACC production and therefore affects climacteric ethylene rise, ripening onset, harvest maturity, and postharvest softening. Evidence type: ethylene pathway gene, QTL/candidate-gene support, functional ripening mechanism. Trait: harvest date, ripening, fruit softening.

MdACO1 encodes ACC oxidase, which converts ACC into ethylene during climacteric ripening. Together with MdACS1, MdACO1 explains why ethylene biosynthesis is central to apple harvest-date and postharvest softening traits. Evidence type: ethylene pathway gene, expression/functional ripening support. Trait: harvest date, ripening, postharvest softening.

MdETR1 is an ethylene receptor gene used in this curated layer to represent ethylene perception in apple ripening. It acts upstream of EIN3/EIL transcriptional signaling and connects ethylene production to downstream ripening gene expression. Evidence type: ethylene signaling pathway support. Trait: harvest maturity and ripening.

MdEIN3 represents the EIN3/EIL transcriptional signaling node downstream of ethylene perception in apple fruit. EIN3/EIL factors activate ripening-related transcriptional programs after ACS/ACO-mediated ethylene production and ETR receptor signaling. Evidence type: ethylene signal-transduction mechanism. Trait: harvest maturity and ripening.
